title: Align Dashboard “Dus (RDV Faits ; Mois Courant)” with Monthly Listing Excel-formula Crédit Négatif total

requirements.summary: Replace Dashboard “Dus (RDV Faits ; Mois Courant)” calculation to use the same monthlyListing.ts Excel-formula total for “Crédit Négatif” (absolute value) instead of totals.totalSoldeRestantNegatif.
acceptanceCriteria: On the Dashboard, the displayed value for "Dus (RDV Faits ; Mois Courant)" equals abs(totalCreditNegatif) computed using the same functions as MonthlyListingTable for the current month.
For February in the same dataset where the February Monthly Listing total row shows 0 for "Crédit Négatif", the Dashboard "Dus (RDV Faits ; Mois Courant)" card displays 0 (not 4400).

file_operations.description: Update the “Dus (RDV Faits ; Mois Courant)” computation to: (1) compute totalCreditNegatif for the current month using the shared monthlyListing.ts Excel-formula helpers (same as MonthlyListingTable’s totalCreditNegatif), including prior-month carry-over, and (2) display bigintAbs(totalCreditNegatif) as the card value instead of using TotauxListingMensuel.totalSoldeRestantNegatif.

requirements.summary: Ensure Dashboard consistently fetches and uses the same inputs as Monthly Listing for negative-credit computation (current month listings, previous month listings for carry-over, and all appointments), without changing other Dashboard card behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: The "Dus" card computation uses previous-month listing data in the same way the Monthly Listing table does (so months that depend on prior credit do not diverge between the card and the table).
No other Dashboard cards regress ("RDV faits..." still matches the Monthly Listing totalDuMois for the current month, and "Revenus du Mois en Cours (Faits et Payés)" remains unchanged).

file_operations.description: Add/adjust Dashboard data dependencies so the “Dus” calculation has: (a) current-month listing rows, (b) previous-month listing rows for the current month (including January→December prior year handling), and (c) all appointments; then compute the “Dus” card from these inputs using the same monthlyListing.ts functions used by MonthlyListingTable, while keeping existing logic for the other cards unchanged.
